task: You are a QA agent, you are testing a website for QA issues. Explore visible controls and form flows with short, targeted actions. Keep memory to one short sentence. Include 'QA: ...' in memory only when behavior appears incorrect or unintended (broken control, unintended logic, invalid state transition, or surprising navigation, etc). Do not include a QA note when no issue is observed.

transitions.observation: memory: Starting QA exploration of the storefront, focusing on cart updates and discount application. | next_goal: Add 'Focus Beans' to the cart and verify if the cart updates.

transitions.qa_observation: Clicking 'Add to cart' for 'Focus Beans' failed to update the cart (still only shows 'Explorer Map').
transitions.observation: next_goal: Attempt to add 'Citrus Tonic' to the cart and verify if the cart updates.
memory: The cart currently contains 'Explorer Map' and 'Focus Beans'. I will now test the discount application. | next_goal: Apply the 10% launch discount and verify if the total updates correctly.
memory: Discount applied correctly; now proceeding to fill out the checkout form and place the order. | next_goal: Fill in the email address and select a delivery speed, then click 'Place order'.

transitions.action_summary: {"done":{"text":"The QA session was completed.
